PixiJS V5 Tutorial (27) - Cropping Images with Rectangle and Texture

Next up we're going to load images through JSON, but before that let's learn how to crop an image. In this post I'll show you how to pull a single piece out of a sprite sheet using PIXI.Rectangle, why your sprites get overwritten when you reuse the same texture, and how copying the Texture fixes it.
JavaScript Core Concepts (26): Objects — Why Changing One Object Changes Another

Time to talk about passing objects by reference. If you don't get how reference semantics work in JavaScript, it's really easy to step on a landmine. We'll walk through call by value vs call by reference, why changing one variable can secretly change another, and the simplest way to spot when reference behavior is in play.
JavaScript Core (25): Why Reading a Missing Object Property Gives You undefined (and Sometimes Crashes)

Reading a property that doesn't exist on an object hands you undefined — but go one level deeper and suddenly JavaScript throws an error instead. This post digs into why that happens, how operator precedence and associativity cause it, why primitives can't hold their own properties, and the two simple ways to avoid the crash.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (26) - Treasure Hunter: Building a Real Mini-Game

Time to put everything together. From here on we'll start combining the techniques from earlier in the series to build an actual little game called Treasure Hunter. This post covers where the game assets come from, which sprites we'll be using, and why loading images through a JSON file beats loading them one by one.
JavaScript Core Concepts (24) — Objects vs Primitives: Why Can't I Add a Property to a Number?

Ever tried adding a property to a plain number and watched it quietly disappear? In this post we dig into the relationship between objects and primitives in JavaScript — why primitives can't take new properties, where those built-in methods actually come from, and why JavaScript really only has two kinds of types.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (25) - Particle Effects the Easy Way with Dust
Time to get into particle effects. In this post I'll skip the native approach and jump straight to a plugin called Dust, which makes building explosions, flames, and star bursts way easier in PixiJS.
JavaScript Core Concepts (23) — Objects: Variables vs Object Properties

A quick note on the difference between variables and object properties in JavaScript. Declaring a variable adds a property on window — but so does assigning to an undeclared name like b = 2. The key difference is that a proper variable can't be deleted, while an accidental global one can. Here's why you should always declare your variables.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (24) - Frame-by-Frame Animation with AnimatedSprite

PixiJS is a great framework for building 2D animations. But how do you actually write an animation in Pixi? In this post we'll use AnimatedSprite to build a frame-by-frame animation, load a sequence of images, play it, and tweak the speed with dat.GUI.
JavaScript Core Concepts (ES6 Edition): Notes From Will Huang's Kaohsiung Class

A few of the more fun things I picked up from Will Huang's "JavaScript in Practice: Core Concepts (ES6 Edition)" class in Kaohsiung. Empty arrays that fill themselves with blanks, var that won't get overwritten, why Number is pure evil, and a handful of other JS quirks worth knowing.
JavaScript Objects: How to Read, Add, and Delete Properties

Now let's talk about working with objects in JavaScript — reading values, adding new properties, and deleting them. We'll cover dot notation vs. bracket notation, why bracket notation lets you pass variables, the gotcha with numeric keys, and how object keys are always strings under the hood.
